title: First Slack message → tenant provisioned → container started → message delivered
evidence: All 7 TC-001 assertions pass (96/96 total e2e tests pass when run concurrently): - ✓ Relay returns HTTP 200 immediately - ✓ Tenant row transitions to ACTIVE within 30s (tenant ID: 3f34637c0fb5a0d6) - ✓ Tenant directories created: home/, workspace/, config/, logs/, secrets/ at /tmp/claw-tc001-isolated/3f34637c0fb5a0d6/ - ✓ AGENTS.md seeded with '## Task Execution' section - ✓ message_queue row transitions to DELIVERED - ✓ Slack chat.postMessage called with "Hello from agent TC-001!" - ✓ Audit log: TENANT_PROVISIONED → TENANT_STARTED → MESSAGE_DELIVERED in order Agent output captured in agent-output.txt: ``` Tenant ID: 3f34637c0fb5a0d6 Team: T_TC001 / User: U_TC001 Data Dir: /tmp/claw-tc001-isolated AGENTS.md: ## Task Execution (+ full content) ```

title: Repeated messages from same user → same tenant reused
evidence: All 6 TC-002 assertions passed (102/102 total e2e tests pass): - ✓ tenant is ACTIVE before second message - ✓ relay returns HTTP 200 immediately for second message - ✓ no new tenant row created — exactly 1 tenant for this principal - ✓ second message_queue row added and delivered (matched by slack_event_id=Ev_TC002_002) - ✓ tenant last_activity_at updated after second message - ✓ audit log contains MESSAGE_DELIVERED twice

title: Stopped tenant → wakes on next message → queued messages replayed
evidence: All 7 TC-003 assertions passed (109/109 total e2e tests): - ✓ TC-003: tenant is ACTIVE after initial provisioning - ✓ TC-003: stop tenant via POST /v1/tenants/:id/stop → status becomes STOPPED - ✓ TC-003: relay returns HTTP 200 immediately when sending message to stopped tenant - ✓ TC-003: relay calls /v1/tenants/:id/start on the stopped tenant - ✓ TC-003: tenant wakes up and becomes ACTIVE - ✓ TC-003: first queued message is DELIVERED after wake-up - ✓ TC-003: subsequent message delivered; message_queue rows are in FIFO order Duration: 4321ms. Agent output captured in agent-output.txt.

title: Concurrent messages to stopped tenant → single start (startup lock)
evidence: All 112 tests pass (3 TC-004-specific tests in tc-004.test.ts): - ✓ TC-004: tenant is STOPPED before concurrent messages - ✓ TC-004: two concurrent Slack events both return HTTP 200 immediately - ✓ TC-004: two concurrent messages trigger only ONE docker start (startup lock prevents duplicate) — docker.start called exactly 1 time, docker.run called 0 times, maxLockRowsObserved ≤ 1, startup_locks table empty after completion, both Ev_TC004_MSG1 and Ev_TC004_MSG2 DELIVERED (2231ms) Total test duration: 12.60s, 112/112 pass.

title: Different users → completely isolated (no shared workspace/state)
evidence: All 7 TC-005 assertions passed in 1691ms (309/309 total tests pass): - ✓ TC-005: provision tenant A (T_TC005/U_TC005_A) succeeds with status=NEW - ✓ TC-005: provision tenant B (T_TC005/U_TC005_B) succeeds with status=NEW - ✓ TC-005: tenant A and tenant B have different tenant IDs (sha256 of different principals) - ✓ TC-005: tenant A and tenant B have different relay_token values - ✓ TC-005: tenant A and tenant B have different data_dir paths (each contains own tenantId) - ✓ TC-005: tenant A and tenant B have different container_name values - ✓ TC-005: marker file written to tenant A workspace is NOT visible in tenant B workspace Complete filesystem and identity isolation confirmed between T_TC005:U_TC005_A and T_TC005:U_TC005_B.

title: Allowlist enforcement → unauthorized user rejected
evidence: All 5 TC-006 assertions passed in 2509ms (117/117 total tests pass across 10 test files): - ✓ TC-006: relay returns HTTP 200 immediately (Slack ack) - ✓ TC-006: control plane returns 403 for unauthorized user - ✓ TC-006: no tenant row created for unauthorized user (polled 1s, count=0) - ✓ TC-006: Slack chat.postMessage called with invite-only rejection message - ✓ TC-006: ACCESS_DENIED audit log entry exists with null tenant_id

title: Tenant deletion → data cleaned up
evidence: TC-007 passed in 314ms. All assertions verified: - ✓ DELETE /v1/tenants/:id returns {deleted: true} (HTTP 200) - ✓ tenant.deleted_at is set (soft delete, row preserved in DB) - ✓ message_queue rows purged (2 seeded → 0 after delete) - ✓ startup_lock row purged (1 seeded → 0 after delete) - ✓ TENANT_DELETED audit log entry exists with actor='admin' - ✓ original data_dir no longer accessible on disk - ✓ archiveDir (from audit log metadata) exists on disk - ✓ second DELETE returns HTTP 409 Full suite: 310/310 tests pass (43 test files)

title: Health polling → UNHEALTHY detection
evidence: All 3 TC-008 assertions passed in 1346ms (313/313 total tests across 44 files): - ✓ TC-008: health polling detects 3 consecutive failures → tenant transitions to UNHEALTHY (status confirmed in DB) - ✓ TC-008: TENANT_UNHEALTHY audit log entry written with reason=consecutive_failures and containerName in metadata - ✓ TC-008: UNHEALTHY state does not affect other tenants (tenant B remains ACTIVE, no TENANT_UNHEALTHY audit log for B)

title: Disk quota → warning at 90%, block at 100%
evidence: All 4 TC-009 tests passed (317/317 total tests across 45 test files) in 1742ms: - ✓ DISK_QUOTA_WARNING audit event written + Slack DM with `rm -rf` cleanup suggestion at 90% - ✓ DISK_QUOTA_EXCEEDED audit event written + disk_quota_exceeded=1 at 100% - ✓ POST /v1/tenants/:id/message returns 507 while disk_quota_exceeded=1 - ✓ disk_quota_exceeded reset to 0 when usage drops below 95% (no new audit events)

title: Idle stop → container stopped after 48h inactivity
evidence: Both TC-010 tests passed in 1744ms (319/319 total tests across 46 test files): - ✓ TC-010: idle tenant (49h) → POST /stop called, status=STOPPED, TENANT_STOPPED audit with actor=scheduler - ✓ TC-010: recent tenant (47h) → NOT stopped, status remains ACTIVE The test file was already committed (`6af89c6 test: implement TC-010`) along with a fix to `idle-stop.ts` adding `body: JSON.stringify({ actor: 'scheduler' })` to the fetch call so the CP records actor=scheduler in the audit log.

title: auth-profiles.json bind-mount → included in docker run options
evidence: All 3 TC-011 tests passed in 6ms (322/322 total tests). readOnlyBindMounts includes `{homedir}/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json:/root/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json`; simulated docker arg ends with `:ro`; env contains HOME=/home/agent, XDG_CONFIG_HOME=/home/agent/.config, XDG_CACHE_HOME=/home/agent/.cache, XDG_STATE_HOME=/home/agent/.local/state; cpus="1.0", memory="1536m", pidsLimit=256.

title: AGENTS.md pre-seeded correctly in tenant workspace
evidence: tests/integration/tc-012.test.ts (3 tests) passed in ~22ms. All three merge scenarios verified: 1. AGENTS.md created from real templates/workspace/ dir with `## Task Execution` section 2. AGENTS.md with section already present left byte-for-byte identical 3. AGENTS.md without section gets `## Task Execution` appended after existing content Overall suite: 325 tests passed (48 files)

title: Startup lock → no duplicate containers on concurrent messages
evidence: All 5 TC-013 tests passed in 23ms: - ✓ TC-013: step 1 — acquireStartupLock(requestId1) returns {acquired: true} - ✓ TC-013: step 2 — concurrent acquireStartupLock(requestId2) returns {acquired: false} - ✓ TC-013: step 3 — after releaseStartupLock(requestId1), acquireStartupLock(requestId3) returns {acquired: true} - ✓ TC-013: step 4 — stale lock (expires_at 1ms ago) is overridden, {acquired: true} - ✓ TC-013: full sequence — mutual exclusion and stale takeover 316 tests passed, 14 skipped across 49 test files. The 2 suite failures (lifecycle.test.ts, tc-002.test.ts) are unrelated EADDRINUSE port conflicts in e2e tests.

title: Provisioning rollback → cleanup on failure
evidence: All 11 TC-014 tests passed (341/341 total, 50 test files): - `provision fails → HTTP 500 returned` ✓ - `tenant status = FAILED after rollback` ✓ - `provision_attempts = 1 after first failure` ✓ - `data_dir directory does NOT exist (cleaned up by rollback)` ✓ - `TENANT_PROVISION_FAILED audit log entry exists` ✓ - `TENANT_PROVISIONED audit log entry does NOT exist (no success)` ✓ - `retry provision (attempts < 3) → returns 200 with FAILED status` ✓ - `provision_attempts still = 1 (returning existing record, no re-attempt)` ✓ - `directly set attempts=2, provision → still 200 FAILED (cap not yet hit)` ✓ - `set attempts=3 → provision returns HTTP 409 (cap enforced)` ✓ - `tenant status permanently FAILED with attempts >= 3` ✓

title: Audit log → events recorded for all system actions
evidence: All 13 TC-015 tests passed in 1965ms (354/354 total tests across 51 files): - ✓ ACCESS_DENIED — blocked user provision attempt → actor=system, tenant_id=null - ✓ ACCESS_GRANTED — POST /v1/admin/allowlist → actor=admin:tc015 - ✓ TENANT_PROVISIONED — successful provision → actor=system - ✓ TENANT_STARTED — start + pollUntilHealthy mock → actor=system, tenant ACTIVE - ✓ MESSAGE_DELIVERED — POST /message with mocked fetch → slackEventId in metadata - ✓ TENANT_STOPPED — POST /stop → actor=system - ✓ TENANT_DELETED — DELETE /tenant → actor=admin - ✓ ACCESS_REVOKED — DELETE /admin/allowlist/:id → actor=admin - ✓ GET /v1/admin/audit → all 8 event types present, total >= 8 - ✓ GET /v1/admin/audit?tenant_id=X → descending order confirmed - ✓ DELETE /PUT /PATCH /v1/admin/audit → all return 404 (append-only)

title: Control plane startup reconciliation → crashed state reset
evidence: All 7 TC-016 tests passed in 1431ms: - ✓ reconcile() runs without error - ✓ PROVISIONING tenant → status=FAILED - ✓ STARTING tenant → status=FAILED - ✓ expired startup_lock → deleted - ✓ stale PROCESSING message → status=PENDING - ✓ SYSTEM_STARTUP audit log entry written (actor='system', tenant_id=null) - ✓ ACTIVE tenant status unchanged Full suite: 52 test files, 361 tests all passed.

title: Config validation → missing env var causes fast failure
evidence: All 3 TC-017 tests passed in 22ms: - ✓ TC-017: missing SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET → slack-relay config throws ZodError (field in issues) - ✓ TC-017: missing DATABASE_URL → control-plane config throws ZodError (field in issues) - ✓ TC-017: ZodError message identifies the missing field name Full suite: 53 files, 364 tests, all passed.

title: Tenant ID computation → sha256(team:user).slice(0,16)
evidence: ✓ tests/unit/tc-018.test.ts (5 tests) 8ms — all assertions pass: - TC-018: computes sha256(T12345:U67890).slice(0,16) correctly - TC-018: result is exactly 16 hex characters - TC-018: is deterministic for same inputs - TC-018: different users produce different IDs - TC-018: different teams produce different IDs Full suite: 369/369 tests, 54 files, all passed.

title: Slack signature verification → valid/invalid/expired
evidence: All 5 TC-019 tests passed in 135ms (374/374 total tests, 55 files): - ✓ valid HMAC signature → HTTP 200 - ✓ invalid signature → HTTP 403 - ✓ timestamp 6 minutes old → HTTP 403 (replay protection) - ✓ missing X-Slack-Signature → HTTP 403 - ✓ url_verification type → returns {challenge} synchronously Signature enforcement prevents unauthorized event injection — confirmed.

title: Interim message sent at 15s when agent takes too long
evidence: All 3 TC-020 tests passed in 24ms (377/377 total, 56 files): - ✓ TC-020: sends "⏳ Working on it..." after 15s when tenant delays 20s — `dmTexts.includes('⏳ Working on it...')` confirmed after `advanceTimersByTimeAsync(15_001)` - ✓ TC-020: interim message sent only once — `interimCount === 1` after advancing to 19999ms - ✓ TC-020: final agent response posted after 20s delay — `'Here is your answer after 20 seconds'` in `dmTexts` after advancing past 20s and awaiting `processPromise`

title: Start endpoint → STOPPED → STARTING → ACTIVE transition
evidence: All 7 TC-021 tests passed in 1505ms (384/384 total tests, 57 files): - ✓ Creates STOPPED tenant in DB via direct insert - ✓ POST /start returns 202 {status: "starting"} - ✓ Tenant DB status is STARTING after /start call - ✓ Tenant reaches ACTIVE status after health polling (max 10s) - ✓ TENANT_STARTED audit event written with system actor + containerName metadata - ✓ docker.start called (not docker.run) for STOPPED tenant - ✓ Second POST /start is idempotent → returns 200 {status: "active"} Full STOPPED → STARTING → ACTIVE state machine confirmed.

title: Stop endpoint → ACTIVE → STOPPED transition
evidence: All 8 TC-022 tests passed in 1515ms: - ✓ Creates ACTIVE tenant in DB via direct insert - ✓ POST /stop returns 200 {status: "stopped"} - ✓ Tenant DB status is STOPPED after /stop call - ✓ last_stopped_at is set on the tenant row - ✓ TENANT_STOPPED audit event written with system actor + containerName metadata - ✓ dockerStop called once with correct container name and timeout=10 - ✓ Second POST /stop is idempotent → returns 200 {status: "already_stopped"} - ✓ dockerStop still called only once (idempotent second call does not invoke docker) The only test failure in the suite was TC-015 (pre-existing, unrelated to TC-022).

title: Container image promote → new default used on next start
evidence: All 9 TC-024 tests passed in 1593ms (401/401 total suite): - POST /v1/admin/images/:id/promote returns {promoted: true, tag: 'claw-tenant:v2.0'} - v2.0 is_default=1 after promotion - v1.0 is_default=0 and deprecated_at set after promotion - IMAGE_UPDATED audit event written (actor=admin, tenant_id=null, metadata.promotedTag=v2.0) - STOPPED tenant with image_tag v1.0 → POST /start → image_tag updated to v2.0 synchronously - IMAGE_UPDATED audit event for tenant (actor=system, oldTag=v1.0, newTag=v2.0) - Tenant reaches ACTIVE status after health polling

title: Message queue deduplication → Slack retry is no-op
evidence: All 4 TC-025 assertions passed in 1273ms (121/121 total suite): - ✓ First enqueueMessage call creates exactly one PENDING row (slack_event_id=Ev_DUP_001) - ✓ Duplicate enqueueMessage (same event_id=Ev_DUP_001) returns false — no new row created - ✓ deliverPendingMessages delivers exactly once to tenant runtime (1 HTTP call to :3100/message) - ✓ Second deliverPendingMessages after delivery makes 0 additional runtime calls; row count stays 1 Test was pre-existing at `tests/e2e/tc-025.test.ts`. Tests event-handler module functions directly with a real SQLite DB and mocked fetch.

title: Allowlist revocation → existing tenant blocked
evidence: All 8 TC-027 tests passed in 1789ms (413/413 total suite): - ✓ POST /v1/admin/allowlist adds U_REVOKE to allowlist - ✓ POST /v1/tenants/provision succeeds for allowlisted U_REVOKE (status=NEW) - ✓ POST /v1/tenants/:id/start activates tenant (status=ACTIVE) - ✓ DELETE /v1/admin/allowlist/:id returns {revoked: true} - ✓ allowlist entry has revoked_at set in DB - ✓ ACCESS_REVOKED audit event written (actor=admin, tenant_id=null, metadata with slack_team_id/slack_user_id) - ✓ POST /v1/tenants/:id/message returns 403 {ok: false, error: 'Access revoked'} for revoked tenant - ✓ no MESSAGE_DELIVERED event written for blocked message

title: UNHEALTHY auto-recovery → tenant recovers and queued messages processed
evidence: All 5 TC-028 tests passed in 1531ms. Full suite: 418/418 passing. - ✓ POST /v1/admin/allowlist adds U_RECOVER to allowlist - ✓ POST /v1/tenants/provision succeeds (status=NEW) - ✓ POST /v1/tenants/:id/start activates tenant (status=ACTIVE) - ✓ 2 messages queued in DB (PENDING) - ✓ UNHEALTHY auto-recovery: TENANT_UNHEALTHY audit written, notifyUser (Slack DM) called with 'experiencing issues', DockerClient.start called, pollUntilHealthy mock sets ACTIVE + TENANT_STARTED, TENANT_RECOVERED audit written, replayMessages called with tenantId, both queued messages remain PENDING (not dropped)

title: Provision endpoint idempotency → same tenant on duplicate call
evidence: All 3 TC-029 tests passed in 1531ms (421/421 total suite): - ✓ TC-029: POST /v1/admin/allowlist adds U_IDEMPOTENT to allowlist - ✓ TC-029: first POST /v1/tenants/provision creates tenant with NEW status - ✓ TC-029: second POST /v1/tenants/provision returns same tenantId, only 1 DB row, no new audit events Idempotent provisioning confirmed: same tenantId returned, exactly 1 tenant row in DB, audit event count unchanged after duplicate call.

evidence: All 4 retention policy assertions passed in 1546ms (422/422 suite): - ✓ 8-day-old DELIVERED row deleted - ✓ 3-day-old DELIVERED row retained - ✓ 31-day-old FAILED row deleted - ✓ 15-day-old FAILED row retained

title: Message forwarding → relay token mismatch → 401
evidence: TC-031 passed all 8 tests in 1652ms: - Wrong X-Relay-Token → 401 {ok: false, error: 'Unauthorized'} - Missing X-Relay-Token → 401 {ok: false, error: 'Unauthorized'} - No MESSAGE_DELIVERED audit event for rejected messages - Correct relay token (with mocked fetch) → 200 {ok: true} - MESSAGE_DELIVERED audit event written with correct slackEventId Overall suite: 429/430 tests passing (TC-027 failure is pre-existing, unrelated)

title: Message forwarding → disk quota exceeded → 507
evidence: All 9 TC-032 tests passed in 1692ms (439/439 suite total): - disk_quota_exceeded=1 → POST /v1/tenants/:id/message returns 507 `{ok: false, error: 'Disk quota exceeded'}` - No MESSAGE_DELIVERED audit event written when blocked by quota - disk_quota_exceeded=0 (cleared) → POST /v1/tenants/:id/message returns 200 `{ok: true}` with mocked fetch - MESSAGE_DELIVERED audit event written with correct slackEventId `E_TC032_OK`

title: systemd unit files exist for all three services
evidence: All 4 TC-033 tests passed in 5ms: claw-control-plane.service, claw-slack-relay.service, and claw-scheduler.service all exist in deploy/systemd/, each contains WantedBy=multi-user.target, and claw-control-plane.service contains Requires=docker.service. Full suite: 443/443 tests passed across 67 files.

title: tenant-shell script validates container is running
evidence: All 5 TC-034 tests passed in 6ms: script exists at scripts/tenant-shell.sh, shebang #!/bin/bash present, contains `docker exec -it --user agent`, validates TENANT_ID argument (exits with code 1 if empty), and validates container is running via `docker inspect` before exec. Full suite: 448/448 tests passed.

title: Tenant Dockerfile does not embed ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
evidence: All 4 TC-035 tests passed in 7ms: - ✓ No non-comment line contains ANTHROPIC_API_KEY - ✓ No ENV instruction sets any API key - ✓ auth-profiles.json is not COPYd into the image - ✓ Only IMAGE_TAG ARG present (no auth ARGs) The Dockerfile uses `ARG IMAGE_TAG=dev` only, no ENV secrets, no COPY of auth-profiles.json. An explicit design comment states credentials are injected at runtime via bind-mounted auth-profiles.json.

title: Docker client wrapper → correct CLI flags constructed
evidence: All 6 TC-036 tests passed in 23ms: - ✓ TC-036: dockerRun constructs resource limit flags correctly (--cpus 1.0, --memory 1536m, --memory-swap 1536m, --pids-limit 256, --ulimit nofile=1024:1024) - ✓ TC-036: dockerRun includes --name=claw-tenant-<id> format - ✓ TC-036: dockerRun includes -d flag for detached mode - ✓ TC-036: dockerRun appends :ro suffix on readOnlyBindMounts - ✓ TC-036: dockerStop passes --time=10 flag (--time 10) - ✓ TC-036: dockerRm passes -f flag Full test suite: 70/70 files, 458/458 tests passed.

title: Health endpoint in tenant container returns correct JSON shape
evidence: All 8 TC-038 tests passed in 209ms: - ✓ health-server.js file exists at docker/tenant-image/health-server.js - ✓ source code contains GET /health handler (http.createServer, req.url/method checks) - ✓ GET /health sets Content-Type: application/json - ✓ Unhealthy response shape: {ok: false, status: 'starting', checks: {openclaw: bool, workspace_mounted: bool, home_mounted: bool}} — no uptime_ms, HTTP 503 - ✓ Source code defines healthy shape {ok: true, status: 'healthy', checks, uptime_ms} - ✓ Source code defines unhealthy shape {ok: false, status: 'starting', checks} - ✓ All three check fields (openclaw, workspace_mounted, home_mounted) defined in source - ✓ Runtime GET /health returns JSON-parseable response with required shape fields

title: Message endpoint in tenant container validates relay token
evidence: All 9 TC-039 tests passed in 155ms: - ✓ message-server.js file exists at docker/tenant-image/message-server.js - ✓ source code validates X-Relay-Token against RELAY_TOKEN env var - ✓ returns 401 when X-Relay-Token header is missing - ✓ returns 401 when X-Relay-Token is wrong - ✓ 401 response matches error shape {ok: false, error: string} - ✓ returns 400 error shape {ok: false, error: string} on invalid JSON body - ✓ success response shape is {ok: true, response: string, blocks: null} (using mock openclaw) - ✓ source defines success response shape {ok: true, response, blocks} - ✓ source defines error response shape {ok: false, error}
